title: Viguiera ensifolia
taxonomy: Viguiera ensifolia is classified within the family Asteraceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as daisies or sunflowers. This classification is based on its characteristics, such as its composite flowers and seed heads, which are typical of the Asteraceae family. Viguiera ensifolia is further classified within the tribe Heliantheae, which is a sub-group of plants within the Asteraceae family. The genus Viguiera is a group of flowering plants native to the Americas, and Viguiera ensifolia is one of its species. The classification of Viguiera ensifolia is an ongoing area of research and may be subject to revision as new information becomes available.
uses: Viguiera ensifolia has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Mexico, where it is valued for its purported medicinal properties. The plant's roots, leaves, and stems are used in various treatments, including those for gastrointestinal issues and fever reduction. In some traditional medicine practices, Viguiera ensifolia is used to treat infections, wounds, and skin conditions. However, the efficacy and safety of these uses have not been extensively scientifically studied. More research is needed to confirm the plant's potential medicinal applications and their associated benefits and risks.

intro: Viguiera ensifolia, also known as the lanceleaf fleabane, is a plant species in the daisy family that is native to Mexico and parts of the southwestern United States. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with health benefits including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects, which can aid in wound healing, skin conditions, and immune system support.
